input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<RefreshView IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
             Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </VerticalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property / Command | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Items` | `ObservableCollection<MyItem>` | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | `MyItem?` | Two-way bound; set to `null` after navigation to deselect |
| `IsRefreshing` | `bool` | Controls the refresh spinner |
| `RefreshCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Calls your API, repopulates `Items`, then sets `IsRefreshing = false` |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Navigates to detail page using `Shell.Current.GoToAsync()` |

**Key ViewModel logic (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**
```csharp
[ObservableProperty] ObservableCollection<MyItem> items = [];
[ObservableProperty] MyItem? selectedItem;
[ObservableProperty] bool isRefreshing;

[RelayCommand]
async Task RefreshAsync()
{
    var data = await _apiService.GetItemsAsync();
    Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
    IsRefreshing = false;           // stops the spinner
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    var navParam = new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem };
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage), navParam);
    SelectedItem = null;            // clears the highlight
}
```

**Important points:**

1. **`IsRefreshing` must be set to `false`** at the end of your refresh logic — otherwise the spinner never stops.
2. **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigation so the row isn't still highlighted when the user comes back.
3. `SelectionChangedCommand` fires whenever `SelectedItem` changes (including the `null` reset), so guard with a null check.
4. Wrap `CollectionView` inside `RefreshView` — `CollectionView` doesn't have its own pull-to-refresh.
